title: MiFID II Best Execution — Slippage vs Reference Price by Client Segment and Instrument Class

compliance.assurance_rationale: MiFID II Art.27 — UC provides continuous Splunk telemetry evidence for this regulatory requirement, uplifted from contributing to partial after review confirmed the SPL captures relevant monitoring signals.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that MiFID II Art.27 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.5.22: MiFID II Best Execution — Slippage vs Reference Price by Client Segment and Instrument Class.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.5.22' running on index=trading sourcetype="ems:fill" (client_segment, instrument_class, fill_ts, price, ref_price, symbol), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: OMS fills + market data snapshot service
spl:
index=trading sourcetype="ems:fill" earliest=-30d
| eval slip_bps=abs(price-ref_price)/ref_price*10000
| stats median(slip_bps) as med_slip, perc95(slip_bps) as p95_slip by client_segment, instrument_class, symbol
| where client_segment="retail" AND p95_slip>50
| sort - p95_slip
description: Best execution is measured by outcomes. Trending slippage against a consolidated reference price by retail vs professional clients evidences differentiated quality monitoring required for policy tuning and disclosures.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for MiFID II compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes trading and sourcetypes ems:fill. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Define `ref_price` methodology consistently; (2) exclude auctions; (3) monthly best ex committee pack; (4) integrate with order routing analysis; (5) document outliers investigated. Indexes required: `index=trading`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=ems:fill`.
visualization: Box plot by segment, Table (worst symbols), Single value (breach count).
knownFalsePositives: Reporting gaps during venue maintenance, scheduled feed restatements, or vendor data corrections.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: OMS fills + market data snapshot service.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=trading` `sourcetype="ems:fill"` (client_segment, instrument_class, fill_ts, price, ref_price, symbol).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Define `ref_price` methodology consistently; (2) exclude auctions; (3) monthly best ex committee pack; (4) integrate with order routing analysis; (5) document outliers investigated.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=trading sourcetype="ems:fill" earliest=-30d
| eval slip_bps=abs(price-ref_price)/ref_price*10000
| stats median(slip_bps) as med_slip, perc95(slip_bps) as p95_slip by client_segment, instrument_class, symbol
| where client_segment="retail" AND p95_slip>50
| sort - p95_slip
```

Understanding this SPL

**MiFID II Best Execution — Slippage vs Reference Price by Client Segment and Instrument Class** — Best execution is measured by outcomes. Trending slippage against a consolidated reference price by retail vs professional clients evidences differentiated quality monitoring required for policy tuning and disclosures.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=trading` `sourcetype="ems:fill"` (client_segment, instrument_class, fill_ts, price, ref_price, symbol). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): OMS fills + market data snapshot service. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: trading; **sourcetype**: ems:fill. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=trading, sourcetype="ems:fill", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **slip_bps** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by client_segment, instrument_class, symbol** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where client_segment="retail" AND p95_slip>50` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Box plot by segment, Table (worst symbols), Single value (breach count).
grandmaExplanation: We best execution is measured by outcomes.
